context: Veeder-Root TLS4B ATG — authenticated RCE via OS command injection; CVSS 9.4; allows full shell access on underlying Linux OS
CISA/FBI/NSA/DoE/EPA/DOT/TSA/USDA joint advisory: CISA and Partners Urge Hardening Automatic Tank Gauge Systems — June 2 2026
Veeder-Root TLS (Tank Level System) management protocol port — default management interface for TLS-2/TLS-3/TLS-4/TLS4B series consoles
Targeted devices: Veeder-Root TLD-350 / TLS-450 Plus / TLS4B — confirmed in EMA advisory (2026-04-14) and CISA ICS advisory ICSA-25-296-03
Energy Marketers of America advisory April 14 2026: suspected Iran-backed attacks on internet-exposed Veeder-Root ATGs nationwide; 15 tanks confirmed affected at one Tennessee chain
Shadowserver documented 1061 IPs with port 10001/tcp reachable globally as of 2026-06-05
8-zero default PIN documented in Veeder-Root TLS-450 operator manuals — primary credential leveraged in hardcoded-credential attacks; change immediately on all ATGs
Hardcoded ACL string seen in affected Veeder-Root firmware versions allowing unrestricted management access regardless of source IP
Authentication bypass on TLS-4 protocol: management protocol accepts function-code requests without valid session token in certain firmware versions
SQL injection in ATG web management DB backend allows direct database manipulation of tank configuration and alarm thresholds
Privilege escalation to root on ATG Linux OS after initial OS command injection — enables full device takeover and persistent access
Suppression of leak detection alarms: attacker modifies ATG alarm thresholds or disables alerts — real fuel leak can go undetected; downstream risk includes fire/explosion and EPA reporting violation

Iran-nexus suspected — FBI/CISA have not formally attributed; CNN May 2026 reporting links to Iran-linked hackers; prior Iranian targeting of ATG/ICS (CyberAv3ngers/Tortoiseshell pattern) consistent; confidence: medium
Energy / Chemical / Food and Agriculture / Transportation Systems — per IC3-260602; ATGs also common in agricultural bulk liquid storage (Ag-ISAC joint alert)
Primary mitigation: remove ATG management interfaces from internet exposure; place behind firewall with deny-all inbound from internet on port 10001/tcp; VPN-gate any remote access
